channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Abortion pills "just in case"? Planned Parenthood will offer them in two states
item.description: In Washington state and Hawaii, residents can now get mifepristone and misoprostol from Planned Parenthood to keep in their cabinets in case they need to end a pregnancy at a later time.
item.content:encoded:
In Washington state and Hawaii, residents can now get mifepristone and misoprostol from Planned Parenthood to keep in their cabinets in case they need to end a pregnancy at a later time.

(Image credit: Don and Melinda Crawford)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Forecasters expect slightly fewer hurricanes than usual this year, but the risk of destructive storms is still high
item.description: Forecasters expect 8 to 14 storms will form in the Atlantic between June 1 and November 30. But the danger is more serious than the numbers suggest.
item.content:encoded:
Forecasters expect 8 to 14 storms will form in the Atlantic between June 1 and November 30. But the danger is more serious than the numbers suggest.

(Image credit: Matias Delacroix)
item.dc:creator: Jenny Staletovich

item.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
item.description: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.

(Image credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez)
item.dc:creator: Diego Ibarra Sánchez

item.title: A trillion dollar question: Will SpaceX's Starship launch go well?
item.description: Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Smokers in China are being chided by anti-smoking women
item.description: Many many many men smoke in China. A small but outspoken group of women are becoming anti-smoking advocates, confronting those who light up in public.
item.content:encoded:
Many many many men smoke in China. A small but outspoken group of women are becoming anti-smoking advocates, confronting those who light up in public.

(Image credit: Johannes Eisele/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Walmart plans price cuts using tariff refunds as shoppers get skittish
item.description: Now that the U.S. government must refund most tariffs, Walmart says it might put its refund money toward lowering store prices. Executives say the cost of gas has shoppers increasingly under stress.
item.content:encoded:
Now that the U.S. government must refund most tariffs, Walmart says it might put its refund money toward lowering store prices. Executives say the cost of gas has shoppers increasingly under stress.

(Image credit: Will Newton)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Susan Collins brings federal dollars to Maine. She's hoping that's worth it to voters
item.description: As Maine's Senate matchup is all but set, incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins urges voters to pick her over Democrat Graham Platner because she can fund state priorities due to her seniority.
item.content:encoded:
As Maine's Senate matchup is all but set, incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins urges voters to pick her over Democrat Graham Platner because she can fund state priorities due to her seniority.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Steve Mistler

item.title: GOP races to fund immigration enforcement. And, U.S. indicts former Cuban president
item.description: Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.
item.content:encoded:
Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Researchers say the Trump administration is finding new ways to punish science
item.description: Even with federal grants largely restored, scientists say the Trump administration is still preventing those funds from reaching them. The consequences, they say, are already becoming clear.
item.content:encoded:
Even with federal grants largely restored, scientists say the Trump administration is still preventing those funds from reaching them. The consequences, they say, are already becoming clear.

(Image credit: Jodi Hilton for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Amid allegations of abuse on Epstein's 'Zorro Ranch,' New Mexico opens new probes
item.description: Epstein owned a 10,000-acre property with a mansion. After calls by the public, the state attorney general searched the property and the state House created a "Truth Commission."
item.content:encoded:
Epstein owned a 10,000-acre property with a mansion. After calls by the public, the state attorney general searched the property and the state House created a "Truth Commission."

(Image credit: Roberto E. Rosales)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Montaño
